Parent Information

Understanding Your Child's Care Journey

Charlie helps your child feel safe. Here's what the medical team is doing and why — in plain language.

🩻 CT Scan

A computed tomography scan takes rapid cross-sectional images of the brain. Non-invasive, painless, takes only minutes. Doctors use it to get an initial look at the AVM's location and size.

🔬 MRI

Magnetic resonance imaging provides detailed soft-tissue pictures without radiation. Takes 20–60 min and can be loud — music selection helps children stay calm and still during the scan.

🩸 Angiogram

A cerebral angiogram uses contrast dye injected through a small catheter to map blood vessels in detail, giving doctors the clearest picture of how the AVM connects to surrounding vessels.

⚕️ Treatment paths

Depending on the AVM, the care team may recommend monitoring, embolization, microsurgery, or radiosurgery — or a combination. Your team will guide you through every option.

❤️ Echocardiogram

An echocardiogram uses sound waves (ultrasound) to create pictures of the heart. It is completely painless — a small wand is moved gently over the chest. It shows the heart valve in detail.

🔬 Cardiac MRI

Magnetic resonance imaging gives detailed pictures of the heart's structure. It can be loud and takes 30–60 minutes, but music helps your child stay calm and still during the scan.

🩸 Cardiac catheterization

A thin, flexible tube is guided through a blood vessel to the heart. It gives doctors the most detailed picture of how blood flows through the heart valves and chambers.

⚕️ Valve repair options

Depending on the valve defect, the care team may recommend medication, minimally invasive repair, or surgical valve replacement. Your team will explain all options specific to your child.

⚠️ Important: This information is educational only. All clinical decisions are made by your child's care team. Please direct medical questions to your physician.
